data.title: Morpho Labs Raises $175 Million Led by Paradigm to Expand Credit Infrastructure
data.description:
Morpho Labs raised $175 million in a funding round led by Paradigm, with participation from a16z crypto and Ribbit Capital.

According to NS3.AI, Spark CEO Sam MacPherson said investors are increasingly backing stablecoin and credit infrastructure rather than decentralized finance (DeFi) lending alone.

Morpho said it aims to become a credit infrastructure layer for banks, asset managers, and fintech companies.

data.title: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif Says U.S.-Iran Deal May Be Finalized Within 24 Hours
data.description:
Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif said on June 13 local time that a U.S.-Iran agreement is expected to be finalized within the next 24 hours. According to Odaily, Sharif wrote on his personal social media account that Pakistan is preparing to electronically sign the agreement immediately after it is reached.

Sharif added that Pakistan plans to begin technical-level negotiations next week.

data.title: Michael Saylor Says 25% of Mag8 Firms Hold Bitcoin on Their Balance Sheets
data.description:
Michael Saylor said 25% of Mag8 companies now hold Bitcoin on their balance sheets. According to NS3.AI, Saylor did not specify which companies he was referring to or how much BTC they hold.

The term Mag8 refers to the “Magnificent 7” group of large technology companies plus SpaceX.

data.title: Bitdeer Reports Zero Bitcoin Holdings After Selling All Weekly Mining Output
data.description:
Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company Bitdeer reported that it maintained zero Bitcoin holdings after selling all of its weekly mining output.

According to Odaily, Bitdeer said on X that for the week ending June 12, it mined 194.4 BTC and sold 194.4 BTC during the same period, resulting in a net increase of 0 BTC. The company said it continues to hold zero Bitcoin.

data.title: Ripple rolls out XRPL AI Starter Kit to push XRP and RLUSD for AI-agent payments
data.description: Ripple is seeking to position XRP and its dollar-backed stablecoin Ripple USD (RLUSD) as payment rails for AI agents, in a market still dominated by USDC, according to CoinDesk. The company introduced the XRPL AI Starter Kit earlier this week, offering developer tools for AI agents to send payments on the XRP Ledger, including MCP server documentation access, Claude skills for wallet creation and payments, and x402 payment support using XRP and RLUSD. Early x402 activity has clustered on Base and Solana, with USDC volume leading.

data.title: Poland’s President Nawrocki Vetoes Cryptocurrency Regulation Bill for Third Time
data.description:
Polish President Karol Nawrocki said he has vetoed a cryptocurrency regulation bill for a third time because it did not incorporate comments he previously submitted. According to Odaily, Poland’s parliament passed the bill in May this year to regulate the cryptocurrency industry.

The bill was intended to implement the European Union’s Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), and Poland is required to complete related legislative approval before July.

Nawrocki has previously vetoed similar bills twice. He said he supports regulating the crypto industry, but added that the Polish government ignored most amendment proposals submitted by his office during the legislative process.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Google Sues Outsider Enterprise Over AI Text Scams Linked to 9,000 Fake Websites
data.description:
Google has sued China-based Outsider Enterprise, alleging the group used AI-powered text scams that led to losses in the millions.

According to NS3.AI, Google said its investigators connected the alleged network to 9,000 fake websites, more than 1 million fraudulent URLs, and 2.5 million messages in May.

Google’s complaint centers on claims that the operation relied on AI-generated content to scale deceptive text campaigns and direct targets to fraudulent online destinations.

data.title: Rheinmetall CEO Warns France May Exit Joint Tank Project With Germany
data.description: Rheinmetall AG Chief Executive Officer Armin Papperger said there is a risk France could exit a joint tank project with Germany. According to Bloomberg, he made the comments in an interview with Welt am Sonntag after this week’s collapse of a fighter jet program between the two countries.

data.title: Michael Saylor Congratulates Elon Musk and SpaceX on IPO, Citing Bitcoin Holdings Among Mag8
data.description:
Michael Saylor said on X that he congratulated Elon Musk and SpaceX on completing an IPO.

According to Odaily, Saylor also said that, as a result, 25% of the Mag8 now hold Bitcoin on their balance sheets.

data.title: China Motorcycle Trade Group Urges Industry to Curb Price Wars
data.description: China’s motorcycle industry trade body urged companies to curb price wars and focus on higher-quality growth. According to Bloomberg, the group warned that imitation and a flood of similar products are squeezing profits and damaging the reputation of Chinese manufacturing.

data.title: DraftKings Says Predictions May 2026 Annualized Volume Rose 34% to $3.1 Billion
data.description:
DraftKings reported in a Form 8-K that May 2026 annualized total volume traded in its Predictions product increased 34% to $3.1 billion. According to NS3.AI, the company said that annualized figure corresponds to about $258 million in actual May volume.

The filing also noted that Kalshi processed $17.91 billion in May. DraftKings launched Predictions in December 2025, entering a market where prediction-focused platforms were already handling substantially larger monthly volumes.

data.title: STOCKS | Serenity Says Foosung May Enter Key Revaluation Window Amid WF6 Supply Disruptions
data.description:
Serenity, known online as the “white-haired stock god,” said in a post on X that South Korean semiconductor materials company Foosung, with a market value of about $1.2 billion, may be entering a key revaluation window due to disruptions in Japan-related tungsten hexafluoride (WF6) supply chains.

According to Odaily, Serenity said WF6 is a key precursor material used in semiconductor etching and deposition processes and is important to global wafer manufacturing.

Serenity added that if WF6 supply continues to tighten, it could directly affect demand-linked supply chains tied to about one-quarter of critical global capacity, potentially impacting major chipmakers and foundries including SK hynix, Samsung Electronics, and TSMC. Serenity compared the potential situation to a “Strait of Hormuz-style” supply bottleneck.

Serenity estimated that Foosung currently accounts for about 10% of the global WF6 supply chain, and said its strategic importance could rise significantly if supply contracts or is restructured. Serenity emphasized that the comments were market observations and hypothetical analysis, and said there is no current position in the related asset.

data.title: Crypto platforms touted early SpaceX IPO access via tokenized shares, but lacked stock
data.description: Crypto platforms promised investors early access to the blockbuster SpaceX IPO through tokenized shares, but the key hurdle was sourcing the underlying equity rather than the tokenization technology, according to CoinDesk. The report said the issue was not technical execution but obtaining the actual SpaceX stock needed to back the tokenized shares.

data.title: Poland to End Fuel Price Cap in Summer as Government Expects Stabilization
data.description:
Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk said on Saturday that Poland will end its fuel price cap in the summer, citing expectations that a conflict involving Iran will ease and prices will stabilize.

According to Jin10, Tusk said the measures helped keep Poland’s fuel prices among the cheapest in Europe during the crisis, but the government plans to phase them out by the end of the summer.

In March, the Polish government cut value-added tax on fuel to 8% from 23%, lowered excise duty to the European Union’s minimum level, and began setting daily caps on motor fuel prices. Since the measures were introduced, they were extended every two weeks.

On Friday, Poland decided to extend the VAT reduction for gasoline and diesel and the price cap through the end of June, but it did not extend the excise-duty reduction.

data.title: Stader Labs to Phase Out Polygon Liquid Staking Token MaticX
data.description:
Stader Labs said it will gradually shut down its Polygon liquid staking token, MaticX, and has stopped accepting new deposits.

According to Foresight News, MaticX has moved into a claims-only status effective immediately.

The company said the MaticX dApp front end will be taken offline on 2026 年 8 月 3 日. Before that date, users can redeem instantly through the dApp at a fixed exchange rate.

After the front end closes on 8 月 3 日 and through the following three years until 2029 年 8 月 3 日, users can continue claiming their MATIC directly via the contract page on Etherscan.

data.title: Poland Plans to Restore Regular Fuel Tax Rates, Tusk Says
data.description: Poland is preparing to restore regular tax rates on fuels, Prime Minister Donald Tusk said at a press conference on Saturday. The move comes as peace talks between the US and Iran progress, according to Bloomberg,

data.title: Shiba Inu Gains 12.6% Over the Past Week but Remains Below $0.000005
data.description:
Shiba Inu (SHIB) rose 12.6% over the previous week and gained 1.8% in the last 24 hours, but it is still struggling to reclaim the $0.000005 level.

According to NS3.AI, the move was based on CoinGecko data tracking SHIB’s recent performance.

data.title: Small Strategy sale tests Saylor’s ‘never-sell’ stance as risk assets rebound
data.description: A small sale by Strategy raised questions about Michael Saylor’s long-held “never-sell” stance on its bitcoin holdings as risk assets recovered. In broader markets, easing fears around Iran and a strong SpaceX debut helped lift sentiment, according to CoinDesk, supporting a rebound across risk assets.

data.title: Former Estonia Central Bank Chief Madis Muller to Volunteer in Police Criminal Investigations
data.description: Madis Muller, who just ended a seven-year term as governor of Estonia’s central bank, is preparing to volunteer in the police’s criminal-investigations unit. According to Bloomberg, the move would mark a shift from leading the central bank to working in law enforcement.
